{{Quote|I just…don't feel I belong here. I thought maybe I did at first, but now…I don't know. I have so many doubts.|Kel Algwinn|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
'''Kel Algwinn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] student at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==Biography==
{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
Sometime before Revan and his companions arrived on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], Kel Algwinn had received a [[Sith Medallion]] from [[Yuthura Ban]] and joined the Sith Academy. His Sith Master berated him numerous times for being too trustworthy and too willing to show weakness. Although Algwinn considered himself weak, in actuality he was not as corrupted by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] as the other [[Sith hopeful]]s in the Academy. This caused him to have doubts about his place at the Academy. When the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] infiltrated the Academy in his search for the [[Star Map]] on the planet, he attempted to persuade Kel to become a Jedi. Algwinn was conflicted by the different viewpoints presented to him, but before he could come to a decision, he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when the residents of the Academy [[Skirmish on Korriban|attacked]] Revan after discovering that he was a spy for the Jedi.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Oh, ah... hello. I don't remember seeing you here before. My name is Kel... Kel Algwinn.|Kel Algwinn|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
Unlike other Sith hopefuls, Kel kindly greeted Revan when the two of them first met. He was doubtful about himself and his abilities and questioned if the Sith were really for him. Although he knew the [[Code of the Sith/Legends|Sith Code]] and could recite it by memory, he could never understand it. [[Kel Algwinn's Sith Master|Kel's Sith Master]] told him many times that he was too trustworthy and too willing to show weakness.

==Equipment==
Like other Sith hopefuls, Kel Algwinn wore a gray uniform. He used a single-bladed red lightsaber.

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Your back. The council said that you would return to Dantooine... I'm glad that you did. I wanted to thank you. Maybe you don't remember me? My name is Kel... Kel Algwinn.|Kel Algwinn|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
[[File:KelAlgwinn.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Kel Algwinn as a Jedi Padawan]]
Although canonically Kel Algwinn does not join the Jedi Order, the player as Revan can successfully persuade him either to do so, or to simply leave the Sith. Either option will cause him to depart from the academy. If the player suggests that Kel join the Jedi before the [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|destruction]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], he or she can talk to him in the training room of the [[Jedi Enclave]], where Kel is now dressed as a Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and wears blue robes. If the player chooses to talk to him, Kel will say that he did not think that the Jedi would give him a chance due to him being a former Sith student, but instead of what he thought, the members of the Jedi Council gave Kel a warm welcome and explained to him that anyone who abandons the dark side were always welcomed to return to the light side. He also acknowledged he had a road ahead of him and told Revan that he had a Jedi Master and that he could not thank Revan enough. If the player suggests that Kel join the Jedi after the destruction of Dantooine, Kel will leave the Sith and join the Jedi but the player will not be able to talk to him on Dantooine afterwards.

If the player is a dark-side character, after discovering Kel's doubts, the player has numerous options on what to do. The player can: tell [[Uthar Wynn]] or [[Yuthura Ban]] about Kel's doubts—after which Kel disappears from the academy, taunt him into fighting and kill him, or speak with him and convince him that the dark side is where he belongs.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}}
